page: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2 threads per core. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing remote Premium Storage. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data shows a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 2701.19, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested systems. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 5398.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3636.0 falling into the bottom 25 percent. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block read-only memory bandwidth is average at 39.23 GB/sec, small-block cached read bandwidth and large-block write bandwidth are weak. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform moderately well, with average scores in Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded gzip compression. Conversely, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are positioned in the bottom 25 percent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8-4as_v5 offers a high memory density per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. The lack of local storage and weak multi-core scaling represent clear tradeoffs. This configuration is best suited for workloads such as memory-intensive single-threaded applications, moderate caching layers, light database operations with low concurrency, and basic text or file processing tasks where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw multi-threaded compute performance.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores, 2 threads per core) operating at 3.525 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core performance, database operations, and memory write bandwidth are weak. Large-block read memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels. The E8-4as_v5 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-concurrency applications, making it suitable for memory-intensive single-threaded tasks, caching layers, and lightweight database workloads prioritizing RAM over parallel compute.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance but weak multi-core scaling
No local storage or GPU included; supports external Premium Storage
Suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads and caching layers
